critical.claim: spec marks test coverage as ✅ Complete (22-check smoke test suite)
critical.reality: The 22-check suite is scripts/smoke_test.py — a live-server HTTP script requiring a running backend (--url or --target=prod). It is not a pytest integration test and cannot be run in isolation. No pytest-based E2E tests covering the full build pipeline (sessions, evaluator, push) are present in the tests/ directory.
critical.recommendation: Add pytest integration tests in tests/test_pipeline_e2e.py that mock or spin up the FastAPI app with httpx.AsyncClient and cover /api/build, evaluator phase, and the git-push phase end-to-end.

critical.claim: spec marks test coverage as ✅ Complete (22-check smoke test suite)
critical.reality: CLAUDE.md explicitly documents '3 pre-existing failures in test_sdk_phase2.py (claude_agent_sdk not installed locally)' — meaning the SDK-path tests are permanently red in the local/CI environment. A suite with acknowledged failing tests cannot be classified as Complete.
critical.recommendation: Either mock claude_agent_sdk in test_sdk_phase2.py so all 3 failing tests pass in CI without the real SDK installed, or skip them with pytest.importorskip and remove the 'Complete' claim until they are green.

critical.claim: E2E or integration tests covering the full build pipeline (sessions, evaluator, push)
critical.reality: tests/test_auth.py and tests/test_webhook.py are pure unit tests for isolated modules. There are no tests that exercise app.server.routes.sessions (the /api/build endpoint), the evaluator phase, or the git-push phase (_phase_push) — the core pipeline is entirely untested at the integration level.
critical.recommendation: Create tests/test_sessions_integration.py using FastAPI's TestClient/AsyncClient to exercise POST /api/build, verify session state transitions, mock the SDK call, and assert the push phase is invoked with correct arguments.

high.claim: spec marks test coverage as ✅ Complete (22-check smoke test suite)
high.reality: smoke_test.py silently skips Linear failure-ticket creation when LINEAR_API_KEY is absent (the _raise_linear_failure_ticket function swallows all exceptions). CI smoke failures on Railway will produce no alert if the secret is unset — a surface-treatment violation per RA-1109.
high.recommendation: Log a visible warning to stdout (and exit non-zero or print a banner) when LINEAR_API_KEY is missing and a failure ticket cannot be raised, so the silent skip is observable in CI output.

high.claim: spec marks test coverage as ✅ Complete (22-check smoke test suite)
high.reality: The smoke-prod CI job (per CLAUDE.md) runs only on main-branch and requires TAO_PROD_PASSWORD secret. Preview deployments and feature branches receive zero automated E2E validation — regressions in sessions/evaluator/push can merge undetected.
high.recommendation: Add a smoke-preview CI job that runs the smoke test against the Vercel/Railway preview URL on every PR, gated behind the same TAO_PROD_PASSWORD secret, so E2E coverage applies before merge not only after.
